recipes.title: Punjabi Chole Bhature
recipes.desc: A classic North Indian and Punjabi dish of spicy chickpeas served with fried bread.
recipes.story: A beloved street food and Sunday breakfast staple across North India, offering a perfect balance of spices and textures.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight and pressure cook until soft
Sauté onions and ginger-garlic paste until golden
Add tomato puree and chole masala, cook until oil separates
Mix in the boiled chickpeas and simmer for 15 minutes
Fry the bhature dough in hot oil until puffed
Serve hot, garnished with coriander and onions

recipes.title: Kerala Chicken Curry
recipes.desc: A family recipe passed down through generations in Kerala, South Indian cuisine.
recipes.story: A family recipe passed down through generations in Kerala, India. It brings back memories of Sunday family lunches and is best served with hot parottas.
steps: Marinate chicken with turmeric and salt
Sauté onions and ginger-garlic paste until golden
Add tomatoes and curry spices, cook until oil separates
Add the chicken and sear on all sides
Pour in coconut milk and simmer for 25 minutes
Garnish with fresh curry leaves and serve hot

recipes.title: Hyderabadi Dum Biryani
recipes.desc: A traditional recipe from Hyderabad, India, a pinnacle of South Indian and Hyderabadi cooking.
recipes.story: This rich, aromatic biryani uses a slow-cooking 'dum' method to trap the steam, infusing the basmati rice with exotic spices and tender meat.
steps: Soak rice for 30 minutes
Marinate meat in yogurt and spices overnight
Parboil rice with whole spices
Layer meat and rice in a heavy-bottomed pot
Top with saffron milk and fried onions
Seal pot and slow-cook on low heat for 45 mins

recipes.title: Bengali Fish Curry
recipes.desc: A traditional Bengali dish made with fresh river fish and mustard paste.
recipes.story: A staple in every Bengali household. The pungent aroma of mustard oil and paste gives this curry its signature flavor, perfectly paired with steamed white rice.
steps: Marinate fish with salt and turmeric
Lightly fry the fish in mustard oil and set aside
Temper the remaining oil with Panch Phoron and green chilies
Add water, turmeric, and mustard paste to form a light gravy
Gently slide the fried fish into the simmering gravy
Cook for 5-7 minutes and serve with steamed rice

recipes.title: Authentic Mexican Tacos
recipes.desc: Classic Mexican street tacos with marinated meat and fresh toppings.
recipes.story: A traditional family recipe from Mexico City, passed down through generations.
steps: Marinate steak with lime and spices
Grill steak to medium rare
Warm tortillas on the grill
Chop onions and cilantro
Assemble tacos with meat and toppings
Serve with lime wedges and salsa

recipes.story: This rich, aromatic biryani uses a slow-cooking 'dum' method to trap the steam, infusing the basmati rice with exotic spices and tender meat.
steps: Soak rice for 30 minutes
Marinate meat in yogurt and spices overnight
